title: nilgirianthus decurrens
intro: Nilgirianthus decurrens, also known as the Nilgiri shrub, is a plant species native to the Western Ghats in India, characterized by its small, white flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Nilgirianthus decurrens include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its ability to act as an anti-arthritic agent. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and the leaves are also used in herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures.
